target.name: Flock Safety

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $7.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.5B - $5.6B.

private.name: Shield AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series G), post-money $5.6B

data.narrative:
Flock Safety, a Series G defense and security company, is valued at $7.5B post-money following $1.4B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—including same-stage peers Shield AI ($5.6B), Skydio ($2.2B Series F), and Destinus ($1.5B late-stage).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier from the last Series G round, implies a valuation of $9.4B. Peer-median analysis across the three most relevant same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields $2.2B, though the peer range spans $1.5B to $5.6B, reflecting material dispersion in the cohort. The peer-median method suggests Flock Safety's current valuation substantially exceeds the typical positioning of similar-stage Defense & Security companies.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $1.9B (low) to $10.8B (high), with a median of $5.8B. Flock Safety's $7.5B current valuation sits above the triangulated median, supported primarily by stage-progression logic but inconsistent with peer-median benchmarking. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set—Shield AI's $5.6B valuation as the closest comparable introduces asymmetric anchor risk—and the applicability of stage-step multipliers in the defense sector, where capital intensity and contract duration create non-standard growth curves.
